data.description:
## Description

**What**: Verify the host-project CI flow end-to-end against a representative downstream test repo. Bootstrap a fresh project via create-dso-app.sh in $RUNNER_TEMP during the verification session, manually patch its .claude/dso-config.conf to set merge.strategy=pr, open a deliberate-violation test PR on that bootstrapped project, and observe that the security overlay fires and blocks merge.

**Why**: SC10 of Epic C requires host-project E2E proof. The plugin's own CI exercises the local-checkout branch of the auto-detect; this story exercises the fetch-from-resolved-version branch on a real host-project setup.

**Scope**:
- IN: bootstrap fresh project via create-dso-app.sh in $RUNNER_TEMP; manually patch dso-config.conf to merge.strategy=pr; open deliberate-violation test PR on the bootstrapped project; observe the Resolve-and-fetch step succeed via tier 3 (marketplace.json HEAD pin); observe security overlay fire; observe CI exit non-zero and merge blocked; clean up the bootstrapped project after verification.
- OUT: host-project E2E for direct-merge mode (covered by Epic A's regression test); fixing any pre-existing create-dso-app.sh defects discovered during this verification (file follow-on bugs).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a fresh project has been bootstrapped via create-dso-app.sh in $RUNNER_TEMP with .claude/dso-config.conf set to merge.strategy=pr (manually patched).
  ← Satisfies: "host-project E2E ... a fresh project bootstrapped via create-dso-app.sh in $RUNNER_TEMP during the verification session, with .claude/dso-config.conf manually patched to set merge.strategy=pr"
- When this story is complete, a deliberate-violation test PR opened on that bootstrapped project triggers the security overlay and blocks merge — verified in-session via gh pr checks on the bootstrapped project.
  ← Satisfies: "a deliberate-violation test PR triggers the security overlay and blocks merge"
- When this story is complete, the bootstrapped project's CI logs show the Resolve-and-fetch step resolved the DSO plugin via tier 3 (marketplace.json HEAD), cloned successfully, and ci-llm-review-runner.sh ran in fetched-assets mode.
  ← Satisfies: distribution + auto-detect end-to-end verification
- When this story is complete, the bootstrapped project and test PR are cleaned up after verification (PR closed, branch deleted, $RUNNER_TEMP scratch removed).
  ← Satisfies: verification artifact cleanup
- unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Dependency-gap] create-dso-app.sh bootstrapping inside CI requires: gh auth in the runner with sufficient scope to create + delete a private test repo (or scratch directory if no remote); network access to the navapbc/digital-service-orchestra-nextjs-template repo; Anthropic API credentials for the bootstrapped project's nested CI run. Done-criteria must enumerate these prerequisites and document fallbacks (skip the test if prerequisites are not met, with explicit log entry — do NOT silently pass).
- [Testing] If create-dso-app.sh's CLI surface or behavior changes, this story's verification breaks. Pin the test to a specific create-dso-app.sh invocation pattern and add a failure mode where create-dso-app.sh CLI changes are surfaced as a clear E2E failure (not a silent skip).

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a project maintainer, I can verify the parity-uplifted CI llm-review works end-to-end on a real host project bootstrapped via create-dso-app.sh
